title: IAM: wire campaign-level AuthZ into BC router dependencies
description:
Full self-contained AuthZ wiring slice.

After basic auth (AuthN) is working, wire coarse-grained authorization into BC routers. Campaigns BC exposes get_user_role via CampaignsPublicApi. Create a reusable FastAPI dependency (e.g. require_campaign_role(min_role=MemberRole.PLAYER)) that composes get_current_user + campaigns.get_user_role and raises 403 if the user lacks the required role. Apply this dependency to all campaign-scoped endpoints across BCs (Wiki, Maps, etc.) as they are built.

Frontend: surface 403 errors as a clear 'access denied' UI state rather than a broken page.

title: Authentication: login, register, and protected routes
description:
Full self-contained auth slice — backend and frontend together.

Backend (IAM module): implement Firebase JWT verification inside modules/iam/. IAM owns AuthN as an internal implementation detail. Expose CurrentUser (Pydantic model with user_id, email) and get_current_user (FastAPI Depends) via modules/iam/public_api/. Other BC routers import only from iam/public_api — never from iam internals. Store IAM-specific data (user profile, permissions) in the IAM Cloud SQL DB.

Frontend: login page, register page, logout, protected route wrapper that redirects unauthenticated users to login. Auth state (currentUser, token) stored in Zustand. Firebase ID token attached to all TanStack Query requests via a default header. Works end-to-end against the backend.

title: Scope and plan Wiki BC
description: Break down the Wiki BC into implementable tickets before writing any code. Review SCOPE.md for Wiki's feature set (documents, folders, ACL, AI image generation, handouts). Define the data model (WikiDocument, WikiFolder, permissions). Identify what can ship in a first slice vs later. Output: a set of new beads issues covering backend and frontend separately, each small enough to complete in one session.

title: Campaigns BC minimal
description:
Self-contained campaigns slice — backend and frontend together. Only what's needed to enable the Wiki BC.

Backend: Campaign and CampaignMember SQLModel models with Alembic migrations, CampaignRepository and MemberRepository, CampaignService implementing CampaignsPublicApi Protocol (get_user_role, is_member), REST API (create campaign, get campaign, list user's campaigns, invite member by email stub — actual email sending is a separate ticket).

Frontend: create campaign page, list campaigns page, campaign selector/context (active campaign stored in Zustand so all BCs know which campaign is in scope). No full campaigns management UI — just enough for a user to create a campaign and navigate into it.

title: Frontend auth flow with Firebase
description: Integrate Firebase Auth SDK in app/web: login and register pages, logout, protected route wrapper (redirects unauthenticated users to login), auth state in Zustand, Firebase ID token attached to all API requests via TanStack Query's default headers. Auth flow works end-to-end against the backend Firebase Auth middleware.
close_reason: Replaced by self-contained auth ticket covering IAM module + frontend together

title: Firebase Auth middleware
description: Implement Firebase JWT verification in modules/shared/module/auth: FastAPI dependency (get_current_user) that verifies Firebase ID token via firebase-admin SDK, extracts user ID and email, returns a CurrentUser Pydantic model. Returns 401 on invalid or missing token. Unit tests with mocked Firebase Admin SDK.
close_reason: Replaced by self-contained auth ticket covering IAM module + frontend together

title: Docker Compose for local development
description: Write docker-compose.yml with all local dev infrastructure: PostgreSQL instances for campaigns and wiki DBs (separate containers), NATS JetStream (broker for Centrifugo), Centrifugo (real-time), MinIO (local replacement for GCS — stores asset images and generated content). Add .env.local template with all required env vars (DB URLs, NATS, Centrifugo API key, MinIO credentials). Health checks so app waits for DB readiness. Backend dev server starts cleanly with 'just dev-backend'.

title: Initialize React frontend project
description: Scaffold app/web with Vite + React + TypeScript. Install and configure: TanStack Router (file-based routing), TanStack Query (server state), Zustand (client state). Feature-based folder structure under src/features/ mirroring BC names. Development server running on 'just dev-frontend'.

title: Initialize Python backend project
description: Set up the FastAPI backend with uv, pyproject.toml with all core dependencies (fastapi, sqlmodel, alembic, uvicorn, firebase-admin, httpx, structlog, pydantic-settings), main.py bootstrap that mounts all BC routers, full module directory structure for all BCs, and shared base classes (BaseModel with id/timestamps, BaseRepository[T], DomainException).

title: Define module structure and project scaffolding
description: Architecture is decided (see docs/ARCHITECTURE.md). Next step is to scaffold the FastAPI modular monolith with the bounded context packages, set up SQLModel per BC, configure the base hexagonal adapter conventions, and wire up Firebase Auth middleware. This issue covers the project skeleton before any feature work begins.
close_reason: Architecture docs, patterns, and issue planning complete

title: Email service: transactional emails for campaign invitations
description:
Full self-contained email slice — backend and frontend together.

Before implementation, evaluate existing transactional email services: Resend, SendGrid, Postmark, Mailgun, AWS SES. Pick based on: free tier generosity (hobby project), Python SDK quality, deliverability, and simplicity of setup. Document the choice.

Backend: integrate the chosen provider. Create an EmailPort Protocol and a concrete adapter in modules/iam/ (invitations are IAM/Campaigns concern). Initial use case: campaign invitation emails (invited user receives a link to accept). Email templates for: invitation, accepted confirmation.

Frontend: invitation accept flow — user arrives via email link, registers or logs in, lands in the campaign. Show pending invitation state in the UI.

title: CD pipeline to Cloud Run
description: Write Dockerfile for the FastAPI backend. Add .github/workflows/deploy.yml that triggers on merge to main: builds and pushes Docker image to Artifact Registry, deploys to Cloud Run with correct env vars sourced from Secret Manager. Frontend deploy to Firebase Hosting or Cloud Run (TBD). Depends on GCP infrastructure and CI pipeline.

title: GCP base infrastructure setup
description: Provision foundational GCP resources needed before any deployment: Firebase project with Authentication enabled (email/password + Google), Cloud SQL PostgreSQL instances for campaigns and wiki BCs, GCS bucket for assets, Secret Manager secrets for DB URLs and API keys, Artifact Registry repository for Docker images. Document setup steps and provide gcloud or Terraform scripts.

title: GitHub Actions CI pipeline
description: Add .github/workflows/ci.yml triggered on pull_request targeting main (not on direct push to main — all changes go through PRs). Backend job: ruff lint + pytest with PostgreSQL service container for e2e tests. Frontend job: eslint + vitest + tsc build check. Both jobs run in parallel. CI must pass before a PR can be merged.

title: Just file with development commands
description: Create a justfile at the repo root covering: dev (docker up + both dev servers), dev-backend, dev-frontend, test (all), test-backend, test-frontend, lint, lint-backend, lint-frontend, format, format-backend, format-frontend, migrate (alembic upgrade head), db-shell. All commands must work from the repo root.

title: Configure frontend linting and formatting
description: Set up ESLint with TypeScript and React rules in app/web. Configure Prettier. .eslintrc and .prettierrc agreed upon and committed. 'just lint-frontend' and 'just format-frontend' must pass on a clean project.

title: Configure backend linting and formatting with ruff
description: Set up ruff for linting and formatting in pyproject.toml. Define rule sets appropriate for FastAPI/SQLModel (E, F, I, UP, B rules). Add pre-commit hooks. 'just lint-backend' and 'just format-backend' must pass on a clean project.

title: Configure frontend testing with Vitest
description: Set up Vitest with @testing-library/react and jsdom in app/web. Configure vitest.config.ts. Add a basic smoke test to validate the setup. Tests run via 'just test-frontend'.

title: Configure backend testing with pytest
description: Set up pytest and pytest-asyncio in pyproject.toml. Add conftest.py with async session fixtures using SQLite in-memory for unit tests and real PostgreSQL for e2e. Add an example unit test for BaseRepository to validate the setup works end-to-end.

title: Cloudflare: DNS and CDN setup for frontend and backend
description:
Configure Cloudflare as the DNS and CDN layer for both app surfaces.

Frontend: point domain to Cloud Run (or Firebase Hosting) via Cloudflare DNS. Enable CDN caching for static assets. Configure page rules for cache-busting on deploys.

Backend: point API subdomain (e.g. api.ttrpg.app) to Cloud Run via Cloudflare DNS proxy. Configure appropriate cache rules (bypass for API routes, cache for public static assets from GCS). SSL termination handled by Cloudflare.

Acceptance: both frontend and API are accessible via custom domains with valid SSL and Cloudflare proxy enabled.

value: Backend stack: FastAPI (Python) + SQLModel as ORM. Not NestJS/TypeScript. One Cloud SQL database per bounded context — each BC owns its own database with multiple schemas and tables as needed.

Pydantic-first validation: use Pydantic models for all data validation — DTOs, config (BaseSettings), external API responses. When third-party libraries return their own types, wrap them in Pydantic models where practical.
